page: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Esv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical Intel Xeon 8573C cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, the processor runs at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and includes a 260 MB L3 cache. The server is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2090.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression at 30.87 GB/sec. However, multi-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 3972.36 and a weak bzip3 multi-threaded decompression rate. Memory bandwidth is also a bottleneck, with uncached large-block read and write speeds falling into the bottom 25% tier. Conversely, text processing and static web serving throughput perform in the average to strong range.

Given its high memory density of 16 GB per vCPU, the E8-4s_v6 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing or high local storage IOPS. It is well-suited for relational database operations, text processing, and single-threaded data manipulation. It is less suitable for software compilation, ray tracing, or large-scale multi-threaded applications due to its limited physical core count and constrained memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv6 family, powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 64.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server delivers strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2090.0 and efficient single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, as evidenced by lower stress-ng multi-core and uncached memory read/write benchmarks. With a high memory-to-core ratio of 16.0 GB per vCPU, this server provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads. It is best suited for database operations, text processing, and lightweight web serving rather than highly parallelized compute tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v6 is an Esv6-family virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, making it suitable for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8-4s_v6 is an Esv6 virtual machine with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and strong single-core processing capabilities.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory-to-core density and strong single-threaded performance for database and text processing workloads.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 4.2 GHz
64.0 GB of RAM providing 16.0 GB per vCPU
Strong single-core performance and text processing capabilities
No local storage or bundled GPU resources
Optimized for memory-intensive database operations and web serving
